title: सुन्दरकाण्ड पाठ (प्रमुख दोहे-चौपाई)
titleEn: Sundarkand Full Path - Key Dohas & Chaupais
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सुन्दरकाण्ड — प्रमुख दोहे एवं चौपाई ॥
(श्रीरामचरितमानस — तुलसीदास)

॥ मंगलाचरण ॥

शान्तं शाश्वतमप्रमेयमनघं निर्वाणशान्तिप्रदं
ब्रह्माशम्भुफणीन्द्रसेव्यमनिशं वेदान्तवेद्यं विभुम्।
रामाख्यं जगदीश्वरं सुरगुरुं मायामनुष्यं हरिं
वन्देऽहं करुणाकरं रघुवरं भूपालचूडामणिम्॥

॥ दोहा — हनुमान का समुद्र लंघन ॥

जामवंत के बचन सुहाए। सुनि हनुमंत हृदय अति भाए॥
तब लगि मोहि परिखेहु तुम्ह भाई। सहि दुख कंद मूल फल खाई॥ १॥

जब लगि आवौं सीतहि देखी। होइहि काजु मोहि हरष बिसेषी॥

॥ चौपाई — सागर लांघन ॥

सिंधु तीर एक भूधर सुन्दर। कौतुक कूदि चढ़ेउ ता ऊपर॥
बार बार रघुबीर सँभारी। तरकेउ पवनतनय बल भारी॥ २॥

जेहि गिरि चरन देइ हनुमंता। चलेउ सो गा पाताल तुरंता॥
जिमि अमोघ रघुपति कर बाना। एही भाँति चलेउ हनुमाना॥ ३॥

॥ दोहा — लंका में प्रवेश ॥

लंका निसिचर निकर पतिउ राज।
मेघनाद सम कोटि लंकेश समाज॥ ४॥

मसक समान रूप करि गयउ बिभीषन पास।
लंकिनी सों भई भेंट तेहि पूछेसि निवास॥ ५॥

॥ चौपाई — अशोक वाटिका में सीता दर्शन ॥

तहँ देखी सीता जग जननी। रोवत जनु कृपनहि कपनी॥
तरु पल्लव मैं रहा लुकाई। करइ बिचार करौं का भाई॥ ६॥

देखि पवनसुत बन बैठारी। बिहसि दीन्ह मुद्रिका डारी॥
जनु असोक अंगार दीन्ह। सीता हरष बिषाद प्रबीन॥ ७॥

॥ दोहा — राम की मुद्रिका ॥

राम चन्द्र कर मुद्रिका मेलि मुख माहीं।
सीता चकित भई प्रभु रूप देखि अगाहीं॥ ८॥

तब हनुमान निकट आई। प्रणत प्रेम बिनय सुनाई॥
मातु कहउँ कपि नहिं बानर। दासु राम कर अति अनुकूलर॥ ९॥

॥ चौपाई — लंका दहन ॥

पूँछ बिनु कपि नहिं उपाई। तब लंकेश बूझि बतलाई॥
लाग रहा मरुताँत्मज पूँछ। लंका जरत भई तेहि खूँछ॥ १०॥

हरि प्रेरित तेहि अवसर चले मरुत उनचास।
अट्टहास करि गर्जा कपि बढ़ि लाग अकास॥ ११॥

॥ दोहा — सीता को आश्वासन ॥

कहु कपि कैसें रघुनाथ। कृपा करहिं कब लहिहौं हाथ॥
मन बिश्राम तुम्हारें आये। सबहीं सुनावहु जाय अनाये॥ १२॥

कपि कहि सुनहु माता मोरी बिनती। राम काजु करिहैं तिहुँ पंथी॥
सत्य कहउँ प्रभु जनम सुहाए। मोहि तें धन्य कौन जग माए॥ १३॥

॥ दोहा — विजय वापसी ॥

राम काजु कीन्हें बिनु मोहि कहाँ बिश्राम।
जनकसुता के भूषण लै हनुमान सुजान॥ १४॥

॥ चौपाई — राम से मिलन ॥

उहाँ राम सुग्रीव सम जूथ। हरषे सुनि हनुमान के बूथ॥
कपि सिरोमनि पवनकुमारा। आयउ प्रभु चरनन्हि पुकारा॥ १५॥

राम कपिहि उर लाय लिये। पुलकित तनु नयन जल भरि भये॥
दीन बचन मारुति तब बोला। मिलेउ जानकी सकल कपि टोला॥ १६॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥

सुन्दरकाण्ड जो करहिं पाठ नित्य नित सकल।
भवसागर कर पार उतरहिं सुख शान्ति अमल॥

॥ इति श्री रामचरितमानस सुन्दरकाण्ड प्रमुख दोहे-चौपाई सम्पूर्ण ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Sundarkand — Pramukh Dohe evam Chaupai ||
(Shri Ramcharitmanas — Tulsidas)

|| Mangalacharan ||

Shantam shashvatam-aprameyam-anagham nirvana-shanti-pradam
Brahma-Shambhu-phanindra-sevyam-anisham Vedanta-vedyam vibhum |
Ramakhyam jagadishvaram suragurum maya-manushyam Harim
vande'ham karunakaram Raghuvaram bhupal-chudamanim ||

|| Doha — Hanuman ka Samudra Langhan ||

Jamvant ke bachan suhaaye | Suni Hanumant hriday ati bhaaye ||
Tab lagi mohi parikehu tumh bhaai | Sahi dukh kand mool phal khaai || 1 ||

Jab lagi aavun Sitahi dekhi | Hoihi kaaju mohi harash biseshi ||

|| Chaupai — Sagar Langhan ||

Sindhu teer ek bhoodhar sundar | Kautuk koodi chadeu ta oopar ||
Baar baar Raghubeer sambhari | Tarkeu Pavantanay bal bhari || 2 ||

Jehi giri charan dei Hanumanta | Chaleu so ga Patal turanta ||
Jimi amogh Raghupati kar bana | Ehi bhaanti chaleu Hanumana || 3 ||

|| Doha — Lanka mein Pravesh ||

Lanka nisichar nikar patiu raaj |
Meghnaad sam koti Lankesh samaaj || 4 ||

Masak samaan roop kari gayau Vibhishan paas |
Lankini son bhai bhent tehi poochhesi nivaas || 5 ||

|| Chaupai — Ashok Vatika mein Sita Darshan ||

Tahan dekhi Sita jag janani | Rovat janu kripanahi kapani ||
Taru pallav main raha lukaai | Karai bichar karau ka bhaai || 6 ||

Dekhi Pavansut ban baithari | Bihasi deenh mudrika daari ||
Janu Ashok angaar deenh | Sita harsh bishaad prabeen || 7 ||

|| Doha — Ram ki Mudrika ||

Ram Chandra kar mudrika meli mukh maahin |
Sita chakit bhai Prabhu roop dekhi agaahin || 8 ||

Tab Hanuman nikat aaee | Pranat prem binay sunaaee ||
Maatu kahau kapi nahin baanar | Daasu Ram kar ati anukoolar || 9 ||

|| Chaupai — Lanka Dahan ||

Poonchh binu kapi nahin upaaee | Tab Lankesh boojhi batlaai ||
Laag raha Marutaatmaj poonchh | Lanka jarat bhai tehi khoonchh || 10 ||

Hari prerit tehi avasar chale Marut unchaas |
Attahaas kari garja kapi badhi laag akaas || 11 ||

|| Doha — Sita ko Ashvasan ||

Kahu Kapi kaisen Raghunath | Kripa karahin kab lahihau haath ||
Man bishraam tumharen aaye | Sabahin sunaavahu jaay anaaye || 12 ||

Kapi kahi sunahu Maata mori binti | Ram kaaju karihai tihun panthi ||
Satya kahau Prabhu janam suhaaye | Mohi ten dhanya kaun jag maaye || 13 ||

|| Doha — Vijay Vapasi ||

Ram kaaju keenhen binu mohi kahan bishraam |
Janaksuta ke bhooshan lai Hanuman sujaan || 14 ||

|| Chaupai — Ram se Milan ||

Uhan Ram Sugreev sam jooth | Harashe suni Hanuman ke booth ||
Kapi siromani Pavankumara | Aayau Prabhu charananhi pukara || 15 ||

Ram kapihi ur laay liye | Pulakit tanu nayan jal bhari bhaye ||
Deen bachan Maruti tab bola | Mileu Janaki sakal Kapi tola || 16 ||

|| Phalashruti ||

Sundarkand jo karahin paath nitya nit sakal |
Bhavsaagar kar paar utarahin sukh shanti amal ||

|| Iti Shri Ramcharitmanas Sundarkand Pramukh Dohe-Chaupai Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
जामवन्त के सुहावने वचन सुनकर हनुमान के हृदय को अत्यन्त भाए। 'भाई! जब तक मैं सीता को देखकर आऊँ, तब तक दुःख सहकर कन्द-मूल-फल खाकर मेरी प्रतीक्षा करना'॥ १॥

समुद्र तट पर एक सुन्दर पर्वत पर कौतुक से कूदकर चढ़े। बार-बार रघुवीर को स्मरण कर पवनपुत्र महाबल से उड़ चले॥ २-३॥

मच्छर के समान रूप बनाकर विभीषण के पास गए। लंकिनी से भेंट हुई॥ ४-५॥

अशोक वाटिका में जगजननी सीता को रोती देखा। वृक्ष-पत्तों में छिपकर रहे, विचार किया। फिर मुद्रिका फेंकी — सीता को हर्ष और विषाद दोनों हुए॥ ६-७॥

रामचन्द्र की अँगूठी मुख में रखी — सीता चकित हुईं प्रभु का रूप देखकर। हनुमान निकट आए — 'माता! मैं कपि हूँ, बानर नहीं — राम का दास अत्यन्त अनुकूल'॥ ८-९॥

हनुमान की पूँछ में आग लगी — लंका जलने लगी। उनचास मरुत चले, अट्टहास कर कपि गरजे — आकाश तक बढ़े॥ १०-११॥

राम का काज किए बिना मुझे कहाँ विश्राम? जानकीजी के आभूषण लेकर सुजान हनुमान लौटे॥ १४॥

राम ने कपि को हृदय से लगाया। पुलकित तन, नयनों में जल भर आया। मारुति ने दीन वचन कहे — 'जानकी से मिला, सकल कपि दल'॥ १५-१६॥

जो प्रतिदिन सुन्दरकाण्ड पाठ करते हैं, वे भवसागर पार कर सुख-शान्ति पाते हैं॥
benefits: Classical tradition invokes this for removal of obstacles and solves seemingly impossible problems
Invokes courage and strength like hanuman
Extremely powerful for fulfilling any wish
Frees from imprisonment, legal troubles, and bondage
Associated in tradition with diseases and removes all kinds of suffering
Traditionally associated with divine protection of both rama and hanuman
Most recommended path for daily recitation in Kali Yuga
description: Dedicated to Lord Hanuman, the Sundarkand Full Path - Key Dohas & Chaupais (सुन्दरकाण्ड पाठ (प्रमुख दोहे-चौपाई)) is a sacred prayer revered in Hindu tradition. Rooted in Tulsidas - Ramcharitmanas (Sundarkand), this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to remove all obstacles and solves seemingly impossible problems and cure diseases and remove all kinds of suffering. It is also said to bring divine protection of both Rama and Hanuman. This sacred text is ideally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Hanuman Jayanti and Ram Navami for enhanced blessings. Comprising 16 verses, it can be completed in approximately 15 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Sundarkand Full Path - Key Dohas & Chaupais in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Sundarkand key dohas and chaupais from Ramcharitmanas in Hindi with transliteration. Complete path highlights of Hanuman's Lanka journey.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Sundarkand and why is it called the most beautiful chapter?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sundarkand (सुन्दरकाण्ड) is the fifth of the seven kandas (books) of the Ramcharitmanas of Goswami Tulsidas (1574 CE) and of the original Valmiki Ramayana, and is universally considered the most powerful and most beloved chapter of the Rama-katha. It narrates Hanuman's journey from the Mahendra mountain across the ocean to Lanka, his discovery of Sita in the Ashok Vatika, his delivery of Rama's ring and message, his confrontation with Ravana, the burning of Lanka with his blazing tail, and his triumphant return to Rama with Sita's chudamani. The kand is called sundara (beautiful) because in it everything is sundar — the hero Hanuman is sundar, his deeds are sundar, the place Sundara Parvata is sundar, and the resolution of the entire Ramayana crisis is set in motion. (Source: Tulsidas Ramcharitmanas; Valmiki Ramayana Sundara Kanda.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is the Sundarkand recited specifically for trouble removal and family welfare?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition holds that the Sundarkand is the most powerful single Hindu text for sankat-nivaran (trouble removal) because it is the chapter in which the seemingly impossible task — finding Sita across an unbridgeable ocean in a fortress city of demons — is accomplished single-handedly by a devotee of Rama through pure bhakti, courage and Rama-naam. By reciting the Sundarkand the devotee invokes the same Hanuman energy of leaping over impossible obstacles. Specific blessings traditionally attributed: removal of family disputes, healing of chronic illness, protection of children, success in court cases, removal of black magic and evil influences, escape from enemy traps, return of lost persons or property, and victory in seemingly hopeless situations. It is the favourite Tuesday/Saturday parayan across north India. (Source: Ramcharitmanas Sundarkand parayan tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Sundarkand recited and how long does the full path take?
seoFaqs.answer: The full Sundarkand of the Ramcharitmanas contains around 60 dohas and 526 chaupais and is traditionally recited in a single sitting (akhand path) of approximately 2.5 to 3 hours, after a bath, facing east or north, before a Rama-Sita-Hanuman image, with a kalash, a panchamukhi diya of mustard oil, and offerings of red flowers, boondi-laddu and a coconut. Common formats: (1) ekadashi/saptahik path — once a week on Tuesday or Saturday; (2) ekadasha-path — eleven times in a single day for severe difficulty; (3) navah path — recitation across nine consecutive Tuesdays; (4) samuhik path — group recitation in a satsang where each devotee recites one or more dohas in turn. The kand is preceded by mangalacharan and followed by aarti and prasad distribution. (Sources: Ramcharitmanas Sundarkand parayan vidhi; Geeta Press tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the major episodes narrated in the Sundarkand?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sundarkand of the Ramcharitmanas narrates ten principal episodes: (1) Hanuman's resolve and 100-yojana ocean leap from Mahendra mountain; (2) the encounters with Mainaka mountain, the demoness Surasa, and the demoness Simhika on the way; (3) the secret entry into Lanka after defeating Lankini at the gate; (4) the discovery of Sita in the Ashok Vatika under the Shimshapa tree; (5) the delivery of Rama's signet ring and the dialogue with Sita; (6) the destruction of the Ashok Vatika and the killing of Ravana's son Akshay Kumara; (7) the capture by Indrajit's Brahmastra and the audience with Ravana; (8) the burning of Lanka with the blazing tail; (9) the triumphant return across the ocean with Sita's chudamani; and (10) the report to Rama and the Vanara army's preparation to march to Lanka. (Source: Ramcharitmanas Sundarkand.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Sundarkand path be performed for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sundarkand path is most powerful on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) — the two days sacred to Hanuman — and especially on Hanuman Jayanti (Chaitra Purnima), Hanuman Janmotsav (Margashirsha Amavasya in south India), Rama Navami (Chaitra Shukla Navami — when the entire Ramcharitmanas is recited), Vijayadashami (Rama's victory day), the Saturdays of Shravan, throughout Adhik Maas, and during periods of Sade Sati or major Mahadasha of Shani or Mangal. Devotees facing severe family problems, chronic illness, court disputes, exam failures or unexplained obstacles often undertake an eleven-Tuesday Sundarkand path with a sankalpa. The text is often recited in samuhik (group) satsangs in Hanuman temples on Tuesday evenings. (Sources: Hanuman bhakti calendar; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between the Tulsidas Sundarkand and the Valmiki Sundara Kanda?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are the fifth book of their respective Ramayanas and narrate the same core episodes (Hanuman's leap, the Ashok Vatika, Lanka burning, the return) but differ in language, length, structure and devotional flavour. The Valmiki Sundara Kanda is in classical Sanskrit shloka meter, contains 68 sargas and approximately 2,885 verses, and is the original 5th-century-BCE composition with Vedic-Sanskrit grandeur. The Tulsidas Sundarkand is in Awadhi-Hindi in the doha-chaupai meter, contains around 60 dohas and 526 chaupais, and is suffused with Tulsidas's intense Rama-bhakti and the simplicity of village devotional poetry. The Tulsidas version is the universally recited one for parayan in north India today; the Valmiki version is recited in Sanskrit-tradition households and southern Rama temples. (Sources: Valmiki Ramayana Sundara Kanda; Tulsidas Ramcharitmanas.)
